locations.name: Truckee River — Verdi / Mogul Area
locations.gold_history: The Truckee River carries fine flour gold washed down from the Sierra Nevada. The Verdi and Mogul corridors have BLM access sections where panning turns up color. Not rich, but excellent for practice close to home.
locations.access_note: BLM land mixed with Washoe County and private parcels in the canyon west of Reno. Use BLM map overlays on CalTopo to confirm access before entering. Do not prospect within Reno city limits.
locations.notes: Good for an afternoon session to sharpen technique before a longer trip. Expect flour gold only. High-clearance helpful on the canyon road.

locations.name: Carson River — Dayton Flats
locations.gold_history: The Carson River has been worked for placer gold since the 1850s. Dayton sits downstream from the historic Comstock Lode. Black sands and fine gold dust are consistent in the gravel bars. Gold Hill hydraulic tailings above Dayton still leach color into the river.
locations.access_note: BLM land borders the river corridor through Dayton Flats. Dayton State Park sections require a day-use fee and may restrict prospecting — stay on confirmed BLM parcels. Verify active claims via MLRS before prospecting.
locations.notes: Park at the Dayton boat launch area. Work upstream along the gravel bars. Best after spring runoff clears (late June onward) when water drops and slows. Inside bends are most productive.

locations.name: American Flat — Lyon County BLM
locations.gold_history: American Flat sits between Dayton and Virginia City in the heart of the Comstock district. Seasonal washes and dry creeks carry residual placer gold eroded from the heavily mineralized formations above. Old tailings ponds are visible from the road.
locations.access_note: BLM open land. Verify no active mining claims via MLRS. Do not disturb or enter any sealed tailings containment areas — they can contain mercury and heavy metals from 19th-century milling.
locations.notes: Bring all your own water — no reliable source on site. Morning only in summer. US-50 access, short dirt road to working areas. Best prospected in spring after wet winters flush new material.

locations.name: East Walker River — Smith Valley
locations.gold_history: The Walker River and its tributaries drain gold-bearing formations in the Pine Nut Mountains and Wassuk Range. Placer gold has been documented in gravel bars along the East Fork near the canyon narrows and below the Smith Valley ranching corridor.
locations.access_note: Mix of BLM land and private ranches along the river. Use BLM GOCA maps or CalTopo BLM overlay to confirm access. Do not cross any livestock fences — rancher relations matter.
locations.notes: Highway 338 parallels the East Walker south of Yerington. Several established BLM river access points. Summer midday heat can exceed 100°F — start by 7am and be done by noon.

locations.name: Pine Grove District — Mineral County
locations.gold_history: The Pine Grove mining district produced substantial silver and gold in the late 19th century. Canyon creeks draining the district carry placer gold that has not been heavily worked by modern prospectors. Elevation keeps temperatures manageable.
locations.access_note: Primarily BLM land with some historic patented claims. Verify MLRS before prospecting. Road access is rough — high-clearance 4WD strongly recommended. Check road conditions after winter.
locations.notes: About 90 min south of Reno via US-395 and NV-338 or NV-360. Elevation ~7,000 ft — significantly cooler than valley. Carry chains or traction boards in spring.

locations.name: Rye Patch — Humboldt River BLM
locations.gold_history: The Humboldt River corridor carries placer gold from numerous upstream mining districts including Golconda, Majuba Hill, and the Winnemucca area. Rye Patch Reservoir BLM land has documented placer gold in the river gravels below the dam.
locations.access_note: BLM land surrounds the river and reservoir. Rye Patch State Recreation Area (improved access) charges a day-use fee. BLM sections are free — use CalTopo to locate entry points away from the SRA.
locations.notes: I-80 east to Lovelock exit, then south ~12 miles. Good candidate for an overnight camping trip. Campground available at Rye Patch SRA. Pair with the Unionville trip on back-to-back days.

locations.name: Unionville — Buena Vista Canyon
locations.gold_history: Unionville was Nevada's first major silver strike in 1861. Mark Twain prospected here and wrote about his failures in Roughing It. Buena Vista Creek in the canyon carries placer gold from the surrounding silver-gold mineralized zone. Creek has seen far less modern pressure than most Nevada streams.
locations.access_note: BLM land in the canyon upstream from the tiny Unionville settlement. The historical townsite is private property — stay on the canyon road and work the creek on BLM sections. Some historic patented claims exist — verify MLRS.
locations.notes: The canyon is genuinely beautiful. Dirt road is 2WD passable but slow (~45 min from Lovelock). Pair with Rye Patch for a weekend trip. Elevation keeps it cool. Bring reading material — Mark Twain's Roughing It chapter on Unionville is hilarious.

locations.name: Hawthorne Area — Mineral County BLM
locations.gold_history: Mineral County hosts the Lucky Boy, Borealis, and Candelaria mining districts, all gold and silver producers. Canyon drainages below these districts carry placer gold in seasonal washes. The Candelaria Hills area has documented placer concentrations.
locations.access_note: Large BLM sections throughout the county. CRITICAL: Be aware of the Hawthorne Army Ammunition Depot — it covers a large exclusion zone. Verify BLM land status carefully and stay well clear of restricted military boundaries. Posted signs mark the boundary.
locations.notes: Research specific creek drainages below named mining districts before the trip. BLM Carson City Field Office (775-885-6000) can advise on current accessible areas. Take US-395 south to Hawthorne (~2 hrs).
